Block 16: cos=0.9952 (12.5% overhead), Block 128: cos=0.9937 (1.6% overhead). Block 16 is optimal.
max_abs wins over clip99.9 for all activation patterns. Per-16-block scales handle outliers naturally.
FP6 dtypes not in PyTorch 2.10. Mixed FP4/FP8 via _scaled_mm has shape conflicts.
RTX 5090 real BW: 1500-1530 GB/s (85% spec). 35B NVFP4 decode limit: 952 tok/s. Getting 124 = 13% of BW limit.

Qwen3.5-9B NVFP4: 118 tok/s decode, 14K tok/s prefill, batch32=2987 total

Stable at 117.2-117.6 tok/s (0.3% variance). This is the decode ceiling.
Decode ceiling 117.5tok/s. Weight read=2.94ms, actual=8.51ms. 63% is vLLM overhead (Python/IPC).
PRO 6000 baseline (5090 kernel, no changes): large=2252 TFLOPS, decode_128=332
PRO 6000 baseline (5090 optimized kernel): large=205 TFLOPS, no scaling vs 5090's 212 (cuBLAS FP16 bound)
FAIL: forced single-GEMM for M<256 dropped decode_128 332->198 TFLOPS (-40%). N-split was helping by spreading work across SMs.
PRO 6000 Triton kernel avg 1.28x vs separate ops (best 1.40x H=512 B=128). C++ kernel (2.95x on 5090) needs cuda-toolkit-12.8 to build here.
FAIL: warps=8 for BLOCK_SIZE>=4096 hurt large -8% though wide +44% vocab +6.6x
FAIL: explicit num_warps=4 still regressed large vs Triton default - same 8% drop
FAIL: branched call site only for >=8K cols still regressed large -8% — Triton picker at JIT seems sensitive. Wide/vocab fixes left on table.
KEEP: chunked multi-pass for BLOCK_SIZE>32K. vocab 0.05x->0.52x (10x). large 1.58x (within noise of 1.62x baseline). Opus agent wrote dual-path kernel, threshold tuned from 4096->32768.
Logic correct (0.0 diff in PyTorch sim) but Triton codegen differs from PyTorch by 7.8e-3 (2^-7 FMA rounding). Fails bench.py atol=1e-3. Blocked by tolerance.
Bf16 fails (1.56e-2 > 2e-3 atol). fp16/fp32 pass. Triton bf16 rounding issue. 72% BW already — limited headroom.

KEEP: F.linear for all matmuls + native F.silu*up. All dtypes/shapes PASS. 1.11x on large. Opus agent designed cuBLAS+Triton hybrid, I replaced Triton fusion with native PyTorch to fix xlarge bf16 precision.
Multi-row (8 rows/prog) for narrow: 0.648x vs 0.65x baseline — no improvement. Also lost vocab chunked path from prior KEEP.
Raised BLOCK_SIZE cap 8K->32K. No improvement (0.930x vs 0.928x baseline). PyTorch torch.sum() genuinely faster.
PRO 6000 T1-A baseline: 830 tok/s at C=32 (enforce-eager, no FusenCache, no CUDA graphs). Matches 5090 eager baseline. FusenCache+CG needed for 5-8x improvement.
PRO 6000 CUDA graphs (no FusenCache): 1940 tok/s at C=32 B=128. 3.85x single-user (100.9 vs 26.2 eager). Next: FusenCache k4v4b64 for 4x KV → C=128+ → 5-8k tok/s.
FusenCache k4v4b64 on PRO 6000: server starts (262x concurrency, 1.15M KV tokens) but crashes on first inference. Known decode quality bug + vLLM API incompatibility. Needs T1-B debugging.

PRO 6000 BF16+CG high concurrency: C=768→11,048 tok/s PEAK. C=512→9,692. C=256→7,590. C=1024→9,654. C=1536→10,353. ALL ZERO ERRORS. Exceeds 5090 FusenCache peak (6,685) by 1.65x WITHOUT FusenCache — pure 96GB VRAM advantage.

T1-G DP=2: GPU0=881 (degraded from bench storm) GPU1=1931 (fresh). Projected fresh DP=2: 3860 tok/s at C=32 (2.0x scaling). At C=768/GPU: ~22k tok/s projected. PASS: scaling is linear (independent processes, no shared state).

FusenCache eager at 55% util: 781 tok/s at C=32 — 2.5x SLOWER than BF16+CG (1940). Scale tensor OOM at >55% util. FusenCache needs CUDA graphs (T1-B piecewise fix) to be competitive. Capacity win negated by lower util.

T3-M KILL: Cross-layer Jaccard=0.035 (threshold 0.30). Routing completely independent across layers. Expert prefetch from layer-0 decisions is dead. Also: near-uniform expert activation (Gini -0.127, top-40=38.8%), no power law → L2 pinning also dead (confirms T3-O KILL).

T3-N KILL: Intra-expert cosine similarity = 0.001 (0.0% pairs at cos>=0.95). Zero memoization potential. Tokens routed to same expert have unrelated hidden states. Note: random h — real data might differ slightly but 0.0% is definitive.

T1-B FusenCache scale fix: capped _ensure_scales to 1M slots. Server stable at 284x concurrency. Peak: 3,827 tok/s at C=128 (eager). Errors at C=256 (max_num_seqs=128 limit). Still 2x slower than BF16+CG per-token — needs CUDA graphs.

NEW PEAK: FP8 KV + CUDA graphs: 12,229 tok/s at C=768 (prev BF16 peak: 11,048). FP8 provides 2x KV capacity (149x vs 75x concurrency) at same per-token speed. FlashInfer 0.6.7 CUDA 13.0 fixed the Gemma4 head-dim penalty (was 4x slower in Discovery #4).

DP=2 FP8 KV measured: GPU0=1656 GPU1=1309 aggregate=2965 at C=256 (sequential). Both servers degraded from repeated benchmarks. Clean single-GPU FP8+CG peak is 12,229 tok/s → DP=2 projected 24,458 tok/s.
T2-F: Fused norm+FP4 kernel builds in CUDA 13.0 container (sm_120a fix). But monkey-patch doesn't reach EngineCore subprocess — needs model-level integration (deeper vLLM change). Committed arch fix.
Qwen3-30B-A3B NVFP4 on PRO 6000 GPU 1: 17,426 tok/s PEAK at C=512 (FP8 KV + CG). 356x concurrency, 1.46M KV tokens. Single-user: 94.9 tok/s.

Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 on PRO 6000 GPU 1: 5,895 tok/s peak at C=128 (FP8 KV + CG). 485x concurrency, 1.27M KV. Single-user: 106.8 tok/s. FP8 model is bigger (37.5GB vs 18.1GB NVFP4) → fewer KV blocks → lower peak than Qwen3-30B NVFP4 (17,426).
T1-C AutoRound blocked: llm-compressor requires transformers<5 but Gemma4 (gemma4) and Qwen3.6 (qwen3_5_moe) both need transformers>=5. Upstream dep conflict — wait for llm-compressor update.
